title: fix(ci): remove misrouted .npmrc files blocking npmjs.org publish
author: Circuit-Overtime

gist.headline: Clearing the npm pipes
gist.blurb: Swept away some dusty config files that were confusing our automated package publishers. The pipeline to npm is flowing smoothly again!
gist.summary: Removed conflicting package-level .npmrc files to fix automated publishing to the npm registry.
gist.impact: Internal CI/CD workflows can now successfully publish the SDK, MCP, and OMPC packages to npm without authentication errors.
gist.visual_concept: A cute pixel art robot with a monitor head clearing out old dusty folders or routing pipes, while the bee mascot watches happily. The pipes/boxes have 'npm' written on them.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of a chunky robot with a CRT monitor head clearing out dusty old folders from a glowing lime green (#ecf874) pipeline. The cute bee mascot hovers nearby holding a tiny wrench. Soft pastel gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Text in pixel font: 'NPM'. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
